markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stone Passage](/Feats.aspx?ID=5378) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Minotaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=788" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 44

**Frequency**
once per hour

**Prerequisites**
[Slabsoul Minotaur](/Heritages.aspx?ID=307) heritage

</column>

---

Calling upon your knowledge of stonework and inherent magic, you momentarily make a section of stone insubstantial, allowing you to pass through. Stride up to your Speed. You can move through stone or rock objects, such as walls, as if they were unoccupied spaces. If you end your movement inside a square that you normally would not be able to pass through, you are thrown back to the last unoccupied square you moved into.
name: Stone Passage

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Shift the Little Ones](/Feats.aspx?ID=5380) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Minotaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=788" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 45

**Prerequisites**
[Friendly Nudge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5364); Expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)

</column>

---

With your large size, you need to be careful around your smaller friends. You have learned to move in such a way that it gives others the chance to make room for you. Stride up to your Speed. If you end your movement in a position where one or more of your spaces are occupied by an ally, each of those allies can immediately Step as a free action so that they are no longer occupying the same space as you. If this isn't possible, you must end your movement so that you aren't sharing a space with an ally, as normal.
name: Shift the Little Ones

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Threatening Pursuit](/Feats.aspx?ID=5381) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Minotaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=788" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 45

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40); Expert in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)

**Requirements**
You are not observed by any enemies within 60 feet.

</column>

---

You emit a terrifying growl and snort, stamping your hooves for all to hear so that they know that you hunt them. Attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) all enemies within 30 feet, and you do not take a penalty for not sharing a language. If the targets are in a maze or similarly difficult-to-navigate location, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to this check, and the range increases to 60 feet. Each target is temporarily immune for 1 hour.
name: Threatening Pursuit
requirement_markdown: You are not observed by any enemies within 60 feet.

    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Begin Stampede](/Feats.aspx?ID=5382) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Minotaur" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=788" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 45

</column>

---

The sight of you charging forward into battle emboldens your allies to follow. You Stride up to twice your Speed and make a horns melee Strike. If your Strike hits and damages an enemy, each ally within 60 feet who saw you hit can use a reaction to Stride up to twice their Speed, but each must end their Stride closer to you than where they started. Each such ally that Strides gains a +1 status bonus to their first Strike on their next turn.
name: Begin Stampede

Calling on your connection to minotaurs of myth, you shunt an enemy into a complex maze full of puzzles of your own devising. Once per day, you can cast [_quandary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) as an 8th-rank occult innate spell, which takes the form of a labyrinth instead of a puzzle room. Targets cannot attempt to use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) to escape the spell (as normal for _quandary_), but they can use [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to find their way through the labyrinth's corridors.
name: Into the Labyrinth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Surki Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5386) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Surki" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=789" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 50

</column>

---

Either through the tales of elders in your settlement or through the messages thumped by those who oversaw your generation dig, you hold the knowledge of many surkis before you. You become trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) and the skill associated with the magical tradition from your magiphage ability ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) for [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) for [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) for [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)). If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. 

You also gain the [Additional Lore](/Feats.aspx?ID=5114) general feat for [Surki Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8).
name: Surki Lore

Surki weapons favor utility and practicality over grace and brutality. You have familiarity with [picks](/Weapons.aspx?ID=389), [light hammers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384), [sickles](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364), [scythes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394), and one additional common weapon of your choice from the [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) or [hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8) group—for the purposes of proficiency, you treat any of these that are martial weapons as simple weapons and any that are advanced weapons as martial weapons.

 At 5th level, whenever you get a critical hit with one of these weapons, you get its [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).
name: Surki Weapon Familiarity

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

While most surkis' ability to taste magic fades once they metamorphose into their adult form—replaced by keener darkvision—you've retained some of this sense. You gain magicsense as a [vague](/Rules.aspx?ID=413) sense that has a range of 30 feet— like all vague senses, it's only about as precise as an average human's sense of smell, meaning you generally can predict only if magic is present; however, each tradition of magic has a unique taste to you, allowing you to identify the tradition of magic present.

<title level="2">Vestigial Magicsense leads to...</title>
[Magitaxis](/Feats.aspx?ID=5396)
name: Vestigial Magicsense

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tunnel Roll](/Feats.aspx?ID=5391) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Move" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=658" />
<trait label="Surki" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=789" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 50

</column>

---

You can more easily navigate underground tunnels by rolling up into a ball. You roll up and move up to four times your Speed in a straight line down an incline. If you reach the bottom of the incline or hit an obstacle during this first turn of movement, you stop rolling. Otherwise, you automatically keep rolling at this Speed during subsequent turns until you hit the bottom of the incline or an obstacle ends this movement (which can happen in the middle of your turn). You're [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) each turn after the first that you keep rolling; if you hit an obstacle on a turn after the first, you and the obstacle both take 4d6 bludgeoning damage, and you stop rolling.
name: Tunnel Roll

You consume the magic of an incoming spell or effect to protect your allies, though you might give yourself indigestion. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the triggering effect; your counteract rank equals half your level (rounded up), and for the roll, use either your class DC – 10 or your spellcasting attribute modifier plus your spellcasting proficiency bonus. If you counteract the triggering effect, you end the effect for all other creatures in the area, but you become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) as your body struggles to absorb the influx of magic.
name: Consume Magic

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 63 **Prerequisites** [Witch's Armaments](/Feats.aspx?ID=4995) --- Your patron's power continues to enhance your natural offensive capabilities. You gain additional effects for any of the unarmed Strikes you chose from Witch's Armaments. If you took that feat multiple times to gain multiple attacks, you gain the appropriate additional effects.
  • **Eldritch Nails** Your nails can tear and rend with the force of a beast. If you hit the same enemy with two consecutive nails Strikes, that enemy takes additional slashing damage equal to half your level on the second Strike.
  • **Iron Teeth** Like a true predator, your teeth can crunch bone. A creature that you critically hit with a jaws Strike must succeed at a Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91).
  • **Living Hair** Part of your hair hardens into sharp quills that you can eject with force. You gain a quills ranged [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) strike that deals 1d4 piercing damage with a range of 15 feet. Your quills are in the [dart](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6) group.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />

**Prerequisites**
permanent unarmed claw or talon attack (such as one granted by your ancestry or a graft)

</column>

---

You've practiced the art of fighting with your natural claws, hooking them into prey and thrashing at targets surrounding you. You gain the following two actions, which let you assume specific stances to strike more effectively with your claws.

 **Claw Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) **Effect** You extend the claws in your hands and focus your attention to take down single targets. The only Strikes you can make are frenzied claw unarmed attacks. These deal 1d6 slashing damage, are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719), and [versatile piercing](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) traits.

 **Talon Stance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=701)) **Effect** You sway in a loose stance that lets you sweep in wide arcs with the claws on your feet. The only Strikes you can make are spinning talon unarmed attacks. These deal 1d8 slashing damage, are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) traits. You don't need a free hand to use spinning talon strikes.

<title level="2">Clawdancer Dedication leads to...</title>
[Abscission Shards](/Feats.aspx?ID=5440), [Claw and Talon Flow](/Feats.aspx?ID=5448), [Claw Snag](/Feats.aspx?ID=5437), [Dashing Pounce](/Feats.aspx?ID=5438), [Feline Dance](/Feats.aspx?ID=5445), [Hindquarter Kick](/Feats.aspx?ID=5442), [Hunting Snag](/Feats.aspx?ID=5446), [Precise Hooks](/Feats.aspx?ID=5449), [Spinning Stand](/Feats.aspx?ID=5443), [Springboard](/Feats.aspx?ID=5447), [Storm of Claws](/Feats.aspx?ID=5450), [Talon Sweep](/Feats.aspx?ID=5439), [Tear Tendons](/Feats.aspx?ID=5444), [Wheeling Grab](/Feats.aspx?ID=5441)
name: Clawdancer Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: permanent unarmed claw or talon attack (such as one granted by your ancestry or a graft)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ostilli Host Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5451) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 70

**Archetype** [Ostilli Host](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=222)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

**Access**
surki ancestry

</column>

---

You've bonded with an attached symbiote known as an ostilli. You can bond with only one ostilli at a time since the symbiote emits low-frequency magical pulses that repel other ostillis. You become trained in [Ostilli Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41); if you were already trained, you become an expert.

 The ostilli is a Tiny creature [grafted](/Rules.aspx?ID=3275) to your body. Like other grafts, it has no Hit Points or Speeds of its own and can't be targeted separately. It can't be removed and dies when you do; in the event of your demise and resurrection, you can bond to a new ostilli during a week of downtime, though you lose any abilities granted by your ostilli bond during that time. Your ostilli is obvious, unless you attempt to cover it with clothing or armor. In such a case, an onlooker can determine you're bonded to an ostilli with a successful [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), Perception, or Surki Lore check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) DC. Your ostilli must be visible for you to use any of the actions it grants.

 Your ostilli is constantly siphoning ambient magic from the surroundings, granting you the Repel Ambient Magic and Spit Ambient Magic actions.

 **Repel Ambient Magic** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Your ostilli glows green as it absorbs magic. Until the start of your next turn, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against the next [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) attack, cantrip, or spell that targets you. This bonus increases to +2 at 12th level.

 **Spit Ambient Magic** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Your ostilli flashes red as it regurgitates a dart of magic at a target within 30 feet. This magical dart deals 1d6 piercing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC). This damage increases by 1d6 at 6th level and every 4 levels thereafter.

<title level="2">Ostilli Host Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cellular Reconstruction](/Feats.aspx?ID=5462), [Chaining Mutation](/Feats.aspx?ID=5458), [Cloaking Pulse](/Feats.aspx?ID=5455), [Deadly Mutation](/Feats.aspx?ID=5459), [Deflecting Pulse](/Feats.aspx?ID=5456), [Propulsive Mutation](/Feats.aspx?ID=5457), [Soothing Pulse](/Feats.aspx?ID=5452), [Spell Swallow](/Feats.aspx?ID=5460), [Spraying Mutation](/Feats.aspx?ID=5461), [Tactile Magic Feedback](/Feats.aspx?ID=5453), [Versatile Mutation](/Feats.aspx?ID=5454)
name: Ostilli Host Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Swarmkeeper Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5463) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 72

**Archetype** [Swarmkeeper](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=223)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

Your body has become a symbiotic hive for a swarm of crawling insects. You can emit your swarm using the Swarm Forth action, and you can use the Bite and Sting action while your swarm is outside your body to command it to attack. Your deep connection to your swarm precludes you from also having an animal companion, though if an ability allows you to have more than one animal companion (such as the beastmaster archetype), you can count your swarm as one. You're immune to any damage from your swarm, and during your daily preparations you can anoint up to five willing creatures with a concoction made from your pheromones to grant them immunity as well.

 While outside your body, your swarm is Large and has a Speed of 15 feet and a climb Speed of 15 feet. It can occupy the same space as other creatures. While outside your body, the swarm can be attacked. It uses your statistics for defenses but is immune to [grappled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects that target only a specific number of creatures. The swarm has resistance equal to your level to physical damage and weakness equal to your level to area and splash damage. Any damage that would be dealt to the swarm is dealt to you instead, though you take damage only once from any ability that includes both you and the swarm in the area of effect (though you take the greater amount of damage).

 **Swarm Forth** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** Your swarm is within your body; **Effect** Your swarm boils forth from your body into a space adjacent to yours. (Its space can overlap yours.) It remains separate from you until the end of your next turn, but you can Sustain the effect up to 1 minute. The effect also ends if you end your turn more than 60 feet away from the swarm. When you use Swarm Forth and each time you Sustain the effect, you can have the swarm Stride.

 When Swarm Forth ends, the swarm disperses into individual creatures. While dispersed, it can no longer be used for actions or be targeted, and the insects return to you and nest in your body again. This typically takes 1 minute unless you're extremely far away (as determined by the GM). 

**Bite and Sting** <actions string="Single Action" /> **Requirements** Your swarm is outside your body; **Effect** Each creature in your swarm's space takes 1d4 piercing damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. At 4th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the damage increases by 1d4.

<title level="2">Swarmkeeper Dedication leads to...</title>
[Aphet Flash](/Feats.aspx?ID=5464), [Buzzing Death Cicadas](/Feats.aspx?ID=5472), [Carried with the Swarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=5469), [Distracting Bites](/Feats.aspx?ID=5467), [Expanded Swarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=5473), [Mobile Swarm](/Feats.aspx?ID=5468), [Pyre Ant Sting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5465), [Sportlebore Choke](/Feats.aspx?ID=5470), [Veil of Bugs](/Feats.aspx?ID=5471), [Weaver's Web](/Feats.aspx?ID=5466)
name: Swarmkeeper Dedication

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whirling Clobber](/Feats.aspx?ID=5485) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=540" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 75

**Archetype** [Thlipit Contestant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=224)

**Prerequisites**
[Thlipit Contestant Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5474)

**Requirements**
You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) with your lash.

</column>

---

After you've grasped a creature with your lash, you swing them around like a whirling hammer, smashing them into all nearby enemies. Make a melee Strike against each enemy within your melee reach. Your last attack must be against the grabbed creature, and you gain a circumstance bonus to your attack roll equal to the number of enemies you slammed them into, to a maximum of +3. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but don't increase your penalty until you have made all your attacks. After your Whirling Clobber, you release the grabbed or restrained creature into an open square within reach of your lash.
name: Whirling Clobber
requirement_markdown: You have a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) with your lash.

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 76 **Archetype** [Werecreature](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=225) **Requirements** You were born into a lineage of true werecreatures or were afflicted with the curse of the werecreature. --- You're a werecreature, able to shift between your humanoid shape, an animal shape, and a monstrous hybrid of the two. You gain the beast and werecreature traits. Choose your werecreature type from the table below. Once chosen, this can't be changed. You gain the [Toughness](/Feats.aspx?ID=5227) feat but also a weakness to [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) equal to half your level. You gain the Change Shape action. On the night of the full moon, you automatically use Change Shape to assume your hybrid shape, and you can't voluntarily activate or dismiss Change Shape until sunrise. **Change Shape** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Effect** You transform into your hybrid or animal shape. Your equipment transforms with you and continues to provide bonuses, but your animal shape cannot use weapons, shields, or other held items and cannot use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) actions. You gain a movement speed, unarmed attacks, and potentially other abilities based on your werecreature type. These unarmed attacks are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group. You can Dismiss the effect to return to your humanoid shape, and you resume your humanoid shape automatically if you're reduced to 0 Hit Points. ## Werecreature Types

>
[Force of Nature](/Feats.aspx?ID=5514) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Werecreature](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=225)

**Prerequisites**
[Werecreature Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5486)

</column>

---

You're a nigh-unstoppable killing machine, with only [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) able to quell your rampage. When in hybrid or animal shape, you gain [fast healing 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62). If you take damage from a silver weapon, your fast healing deactivates until the end of your next turn.
name: Force of Nature

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=572" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

Fighting in the wilds has honed your understanding of the unique abilities many creatures have. You're trained in [Wild Mimic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), a special Lore skill that can be used only to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about creatures to learn their abilities. If you have legendary proficiency in Nature, you gain expert proficiency in Wild Mimic Lore, but you can't increase your proficiency in Wild Mimic Lore by any other means. When you succeed at a Wild Mimic Lore to Recall Knowledge, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your saving throws against the next attack or ability that the subject of your Recall Knowledge targets you with.

<title level="2">Wild Mimic Dedication leads to...</title>
[Electrogenesis](/Feats.aspx?ID=5518), [Emergency Regeneration](/Feats.aspx?ID=5526), [Environmental Adaptability](/Feats.aspx?ID=5521), [Ferocity Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5516), [Petrifying Gaze Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5522), [Pounce Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5525), [Primal Proportions](/Feats.aspx?ID=5519), [Rend Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5517), [Roar Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5524), [Trample Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5523), [Wild Speech](/Feats.aspx?ID=5520)
name: Wild Mimic Dedication

[Ferocity Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5516) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226)

**Frequency**
once per day

**Prerequisites**
[Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have encountered a creature who has kept fighting or dealt damage after being reduced to 0 Hit Points (such as via death throes or [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)) or have identified a creature with such an ability in combat

**Trigger**
You would be reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed.

</column>

---

You have trained yourself to continue fighting even when your body is screaming for you to stop. You avoid being knocked out and remain at 1 Hit Point, and your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition increases by 1.

[Rend Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5517) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 80

**Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226)

**Prerequisites**
[Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature use [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73) or have identified a creature that has Rend in combat

**Requirements**
You have hit the same enemy with two consecutive melee Strikes that each dealt slashing damage.

---

Taking advantage of the brutal efficiency of hacking away at a target with two slashing attacks, you've mastered the art of punishing a foe caught between your slices. You deal 1d6 slashing damage to the target. This damage increases to 2d6 at 10th level and to 3d6 at 16th level.

prerequisite_markdown: [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature use [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73) or have identified a creature that has Rend in combat

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 80 **Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature deal electricity damage with an unarmed Strike or have identified a creature capable of dealing electricity damage with an unarmed Strike in combat --- Some animals and beasts have electric organs in their body that they can use to jolt their prey. You may not have the requisite physiology to do the same, but a touch of primal magic can achieve the same effect. Make a melee [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) Strike against a creature. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 1d12 electricity damage, and the creature must make a Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is clumsy 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is clumsy 3 for 1 round.
name: Electrogenesis
prerequisite_markdown: [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature deal electricity damage with an unarmed Strike or have identified a creature capable of dealing electricity damage with an unarmed Strike in combat

</title>

<traits>

[Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515)

</column>

---

Having experienced the harshest environs that nature has to offer, your body is able to adapt to intense heat and cold. During your daily preparations, you can choose to become protected from the effects of [severe cold or severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) until the next time you make your daily preparations. Starting at 12th level, this activity also protects you from the effects of extreme cold or extreme heat.
name: Environmental Adaptability

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 81 **Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226) **Prerequisites** [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have attempted a saving throw against a creature’s petrifying gaze or a similar ability or have identified a creature with such an ability in combat --- Either by witnessing or surviving the petrifying stare of a [medusa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3096) or similar creature, you have learned to petrify those you lock eyes with. You stare at a creature you can see within 30 feet. The creature attempts a Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. You must wait 1d4 rounds before using this ability again. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to your Petrifying Gaze Mimicry for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 1 for 1 round and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296).
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round and immobilized until it Escapes.
name: Petrifying Gaze Mimicry
prerequisite_markdown: [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have attempted a saving throw against a creature’s petrifying gaze or a similar ability or have identified a creature with such an ability in combat

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Trample Mimicry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5523) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=535" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226)

**Prerequisites**
[Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature use [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) or have identified a creature with Trample in combat

</column>

---

You Stride up to double your speed and can move through the spaces of creatures at least one size smaller, trampling each creature whose space you enter. You can attempt to trample the same creature only once in a single use of Trample Mimicry. You deal 3d12 bludgeoning damage to each trampled creature ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC). This damage increases by 1d12 at levels 14 and 18.
name: Trample Mimicry
prerequisite_markdown: [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have seen a creature use [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) or have identified a creature with Trample in combat

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 81 **Archetype** [Wild Mimic](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=226) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes **Prerequisites** [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have been targeted by a creature’s [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) ability that effects an area or have identified a creature with an auditory ability that effects an area --- You unleash a roar so loud that it's felt as deeply as it's heard. All creatures within a 15-foot emanation take 8d6 sonic damage and must attempt a Fortitude save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. This damage increases by 2d6 at 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93). If the creature is adjacent to you, they're also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is stunned 2. If the creature is adjacent to you, they're also deafened for 1 minute.
name: Roar Mimicry
prerequisite_markdown: [Wild Mimic Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=5515); you have been targeted by a creature’s [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) ability that effects an area or have identified a creature with an auditory ability that effects an area

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 81

---

You have mastered the art of stalking your prey and striking when they are most vulnerable. You Stride and then Strike. If you began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), you remain hidden until after the Strike. You can use Pounce Mimicry while [Burrowing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.
